name: chokepoint-oauth-device-code
bodyMarkdown:
# OAuth Device Code Phishing via Auth Broker — Detection Chokepoint

**Priority:** HIGH | **Tactic:** Defense Evasion | **Difficulty:** LOW | **Prevalence:** MEDIUM
**Techniques:** T1550.001

## Overview
Abuse OAuth device authorization grant to obtain M365 tokens without credential-harvest page. Attacker uses MAB app (client ID 29d9ed98) — generates distinctive authenticationProtocol:deviceCode signal.

## Chokepoint Stages

### 1. Device Code Redemption via MAB App
Victim enters code at microsoft.com/devicelogin.
Observable: SigninLogs: authenticationProtocol=deviceCode AND appId=29d9ed98 AND resultType=0 AND isInteractive=true.

### 2. Token Inheritance via FOCI
Kit exchanges MAB refresh token for Exchange/Graph/SharePoint/Teams tokens.
Observable: NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs: multiple resource sign-ins from cloud-VPS ASN.

## Detection Logic
- **Hunt:** Device-code flow on MAB app ID (rare in managed tenants)
- **Analyst:** MAB device-code + isInteractive = high-confidence phishing
